1.reason: [Error: When encountering '&', the code reads the next character but does not check if it is '&'. If the next character is not '&', it should output an error (type 'a') and not output "&&". However, the code unconditionally outputs "&&" without verifying the next character, which will incorrectly treat a single '&' as "&&".]

402.reason: Consumes the next character from the stream. If it is '&', the main parser will miss it. Should use a temporary variable or fseek back.

387.reason: [This code assumes that every '&' is followed by another '&', but if a single '&' appears (which is a lexical error), it will still consume the next character and output "&&" as token AND, instead of detecting the error. This bypasses the error detection logic in `jiancha()` because `dealtoken` is only called after `jiancha()` passes, but `jiancha()` may not catch all cases due to separate scanning. Moreover, during normal tokenization, encountering a single '&' should trigger error 'a', but this code does not check if the next character is actually '&'.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This code assumes that every '&' is followed by another '&', but if a single '&' appears (which is a lexical error), it will still consume the next character and output "&&" as token AND, instead of detecting the error. This bypasses the error detection logic in `jiancha()` because `dealtoken` is only called after `jiancha()` passes, but `jiancha()` may not catch all cases due to separate scanning. Moreover, during normal tokenization, encountering a single '&' should trigger error 'a', but this code does not check if the next character is actually '&'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This code assumes that every '&' is followed by another '&', but if a single '&' appears (which is a lexical error), it will incorrectly consume the next character and output "&&" as a valid token, instead of detecting the error. This bypasses the error checking done in jiancha() because jiancha() runs separately and doesn't affect tokenization logic in dealtoken().]

392.reason: [Same issue as above: if a single '|' is encountered, this code blindly consumes the next character and outputs "||", even if the next character is not '|', leading to incorrect tokenization instead of reporting error type 'a'.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Similarly, this assumes every '|' is followed by another '|'. If a single '|' appears, it will incorrectly consume the next character and output "||", failing to report error type 'a' during tokenization. The error detection in jiancha() may run first, but if kongzhi is not set properly or input changes between checks, this tokenization path can still produce wrong output.]
